attacking_style: Long-range solo attacks (40+km) on descents or early climbs, leveraging superior bike-handling and sustained power rather than explosive accelerations. Targets breakaway scenarios more than bunch finishes.
team_tactics: INEOS uses him flexibly: early bridging on cobbles/climbs to establish platforms, then freedom to attack from breaks. Sacrificed for team leaders (Rodríguez 2023) when GC priorities dictate.
terrain_preference: Technical descents and mixed terrain (Strade Bianche, cobbled classics). Competitive but not elite on steep Monument-grade climbs; needs race fragmentation rather than pure climbing duels.
vulnerability: Lacks explosive acceleration on steepest finale climbs against pure climbers (Pogačar, Remco). Form inconsistency evident across seasons (crashed/dropped Tirreno 2023, Fleche Wallonne 2022).
key_patterns: Attacks from breakaways on final climbs, not bunch finishes (Tour stage wins, Alpe d'Huez).
Executes ultra-long solo efforts 40-50km from finish when chasers hesitate behind (Strade Bianche 2023).
Bridges aggressively early on technical/cobbled sections to establish winning platforms (Dwars door Vlaanderen, Strade Bianche).
Strong Monument podium results but lacks winning punch on steepest finales (LBL 2nd, Amstel 3rd).
Serves domestique role when team GC needs dictate, burning matches for leaders (Tour 2023 for Rodríguez).
form_trajectory: neutral: Monument podiums and Tour stage win 2023 show high ceiling, but Tirreno crash/struggle and domestique duties suggest role uncertainty and form peaks inconsistently
